task: If I'm 30, plan to retire at 65, and can save $300/month, with a 3% annual return, 13% current tax rate, and 24% retirement tax rate, show the comparison chart between Traditional and Roth IRA.
final_result_response:
The IRA comparison chart has been successfully displayed on Chase's IRA calculator (https://www.chase.com/personal/investments/retirement/retirement-calculators/traditional-ira-calculator) with the following parameters:

**Input Parameters:**
- Age Range: 30 to 65
- Starting balance: $0
- Monthly contribution: $300
- Annual rate of return: 3%
- Current tax rate: 13%
- Estimated tax rate at retirement: 24%

**Comparison Results at Retirement (Year 2061):**

| Account Type | Estimated Value |
|---|---|
| **Roth IRA** | **$224,000** (highest) |
| General (taxable) | $207,000 |
| Non-deductible Traditional IRA | $201,000 |
| Deductible Traditional IRA | $197,000 (lowest) |

**Key Takeaway:** The **Roth IRA comes out on top by a significant margin ($224k)**, outperforming the Deductible Traditional IRA ($197k) by **$27,000**. This is because your retirement tax rate (24%) is substantially higher than your current tax rate (13%), making it more advantageous to pay taxes now (Roth) rather than defer them to retirement (Traditional).

Now let me scroll down to see the comparison chart between Traditional and Roth IRA.

I can now see the comparison chart with all parameters correctly set. The chart shows the "Estimated account value" over time (2027–2061) with four lines comparing different IRA types. Let me scroll down a bit more to see the full legend and any additional comparison data.

I can now see the complete comparison chart and results. The chart shows the estimated account values at retirement (year 2061) for all four account types with the specified parameters. Let me take a clean screenshot of the full chart area by scrolling back up slightly.

The comparison chart is now fully visible with all the correct parameters. Here's the complete view showing:

- **Current tax rate: 13%** and **Estimated tax rate at retirement: 24%** (visible at the top)
- The **"Estimated account value"** chart spanning from 2027 to 2061
- The four account types compared at the bottom with their final values:
  - **Roth IRA: $224k** (highest - purple square, top line)
  - **General (taxable): $207k** (dark blue circle)
  - **Non-deductible Traditional IRA: $201k** (orange triangle)
  - **Deductible Traditional IRA: $197k** (green diamond, lowest)

The chart clearly shows that with these parameters (30 years old, retiring at 65, $300/month, 3% return, 13% current tax rate, and 24% retirement tax rate), the **Roth IRA significantly outperforms the Traditional IRA** — because your retirement tax rate (24%) is higher than your current tax rate (13%), making it better to pay taxes now (Roth) rather than deferring them (Traditional).
